LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND JOSE CISNEROS, TREASUER OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF LACKS STANDING TO SUE. COURT MAY NOT GRANT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF TO PREVENT TAX COLLECTION. (SEE DARR VS. ALVORD (1980) 101 CAL. APP. 3D 480). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PLEAD HE IS INJURED OR WILL BE INJURED BY PROPOSITION Q. ANY PARTY WHO MAY BE INJURED BY PROPOSITION Q HAS AN ADEQUATE REMEDY TO CHALLENGE ITS VALIDITY BY PAYING THE TAX FIRST AND THEN BRINGING A CLAIM FOR REFUND BEFORE SEEKING JUDICIAL RELIEF. LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED. THE TEXT OF THE AMENDMENTS TO SECTION 902.1(D) WAS INCLUDED IN THE VOTER PAMPHLET, AND THE EFFECT OF PROPOSITION Q WAS ADEQUATELY EXPLAINED TO VOTERS. NO REASONABLE LIKELIHOOD THAT PLAINTIFF WILL BE ABLE TO STATE A VALID CAUSE OF ACTION. (ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT). JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2009 Off Calendar

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant City and County of San Francisco, and Jose Cisneros, Treasuerr of the City and County of San Francisco's Demurrer to Complaint. Appearances: Amy L. Silverstein, Esq. and Amy L. Silverstein, Esq. of Silverstein & Pomerantz LLP (415) 593-3502, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; James M. Emery, Chief of Complex Litigation, CCSF, (415) 554-3800, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff lacks standing to sue. Court may not grant injunctive relief to prevent tax collection. (See Darr vs. Alvord (1980) 101 Cal. App. 3d 480). Plaintiff fails to plead he is injured or will be injured by Proposition Q. Any party who may be injured by Proposition Q has an adequate remedy to challenge its validity by paying the tax first and then bringing a claim for refund before seeking judicial relief. Leave to amend is denied. The text of the amendments to section 902.1(d) was included in the Voter pamphlet, and the effect of Proposition Q was adequately explained to voters. No reasonable likelihood that plaintiff will be able to state a valid cause of action. (Order signed in open Court).302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: To be heard on June 11, 2009. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
